pr.title: remove verbosity flags from elf2dol call
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Small build-system cleanup in the repository root Makefile: the DOL build rule stopped passing two verbose flags to elf2dol. The command changed from `$(ELF2DOL) -v -v $< $@ $(SDATA_PDHR) $(SBSS_PDHR)` to `$(ELF2DOL) $< $@ $(SDATA_PDHR) $(SBSS_PDHR)`, while preserving the same ELF input, DOL output, SDATA/SBSS program-header arguments, and SHA1 verification step.
what_changed: In `Makefile`, the `$(DOL): $(ELF) | tools` recipe removed `-v -v` from the `$(ELF2DOL)` invocation.
The `elf2dol` positional arguments remained unchanged: input ELF `$<`, output DOL `$@`, `$(SDATA_PDHR)`, and `$(SBSS_PDHR)`.
smart_moves: Kept the fix narrowly scoped to the noisy tool invocation instead of changing build products, dependencies, or verification logic.
Preserved the SHA1 check after DOL generation, so removing verbosity did not weaken output validation.
Left the existing Makefile variables and positional argument order intact, reducing risk in a build-rule change.
decomp_lessons: For decomp build rules, prefer quiet/reproducible tool invocations unless verbose output is needed for debugging.
When adjusting tool flags, avoid disturbing artifact inputs, outputs, section/program-header arguments, or checksum verification.
Small Makefile cleanups can improve build log signal-to-noise without changing matching behavior, provided generated artifacts remain checked.
naming_conventions: Build variables used in this rule are uppercase Makefile-style names: `ELF2DOL`, `DOL`, `ELF`, `SDATA_PDHR`, `SBSS_PDHR`, `SHA1SUM`, and `TARGET`.
The program-header-related variables use `_PDHR` suffixes, as shown by `SDATA_PDHR` and `SBSS_PDHR`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function matching tactic is evidenced; this PR only changed the Makefile invocation of `elf2dol`.
The unchanged SHA1 verification indicates the intended safeguard was to confirm the resulting DOL still matched the expected target hash after the tool call.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `$(ELF2DOL)` or adding/removing verbosity/debug flags in the Makefile.
Check whether `elf2dol` changed its CLI around this time, making `-v -v` unnecessary or undesirable.
Search for build failures or CI log noise related to `elf2dol -v -v` before PR 35.
